text: L-Tyrosine (L-Tyr), chemical formula C9H11NO3, molecular weight 181.19, is a pure organic molecular crystal, a natural aromatic amino acid with a hydroxyl group on the phenolic ring. It crystallizes in the non-centrosymmetric, chiral orthorhombic space group P212121 (No. 19), with unit cell a = 6.913 Å, b = 21.116 Å, c = 5.829 Å, Z = 4, volume 850.888 Å³, determined by single-crystal X-ray diffraction at 295 K with an R factor of 4.9% (CCDC 1208549, CSD refcode LTYROS10). L-Tyr exhibits unusually low solubility in water (∼0.5 mg/mL) despite being more polar than L-phenylalanine, a property attributed to strong face-to-face dimer formation via hydroxyl-mediated hydrogen bonds creating a three-dimensional network. In a 2019 study, L-Tyr crystals demonstrated the highest thermal stability (Tm = 312 °C) and mechanical rigidity (Young's modulus 177.03 ± 54.39 GPa) among three aromatic amino acids. Computationally predicted DFT piezoelectric strain constants range from 5.0 to 9.7 pC/N, comparable to inorganic materials ZnO and CdS. An L-Tyr crystal-film-based nanogenerator produced high stable piezoelectric outputs of 0.5 V open-circuit voltage and 35 nA short-circuit current at 31 N applied force. The rigid needle crystals exhibited active optical waveguiding properties, and L-Tyr/PDMS composite devices showed mechano-responsive bending and potential for soft robotics and photonic applications.
experimental_method: Piezoelectric nanogenerator (sandwich device, open-circuit voltage and short-circuit current measurement)
property_ref_title: Rigid Tightly Packed Amino Acid Crystals as Functional Supramolecular Materials
